actions.description: Delete a tweet by ID (must be authored by the authenticated user)
actions.name: delete-tweet

tweet_id.description: The ID of the tweet to delete

actions.description: Get Twitter user profile information by username or for the authenticated user
actions.name: get-user-info

username.description: Twitter username (without @). If omitted, returns the authenticated user.

actions.description: Get recent tweets from a user timeline
actions.name: get-user-tweets

username.description: Twitter username (without @). If omitted, returns authenticated user tweets.

max_results.description: Number of tweets to return (5-100, default 10)

actions.description: Post a tweet on behalf of the authenticated user
actions.name: post-tweet

actions.description: Search recent tweets matching a query (last 7 days)
actions.name: search-tweets

query.description: Twitter search query (supports operators like from:, to:, has:, is:)

actions.description: Like a tweet on behalf of the authenticated user
actions.name: like-tweet

tweet_id.description: The ID of the tweet to like

actions.description: Unlike a previously liked tweet
actions.name: unlike-tweet

tweet_id.description: The ID of the tweet to unlike

actions.description: Retweet a tweet on behalf of the authenticated user
actions.name: retweet

tweet_id.description: The ID of the tweet to retweet

actions.description: Undo a retweet
actions.name: unretweet

tweet_id.description: The ID of the tweet to unretweet

actions.description: Get a single tweet by ID with full metrics
actions.name: get-tweet

tweet_id.description: The ID of the tweet to retrieve

actions.description: Get recent mentions of the authenticated user
actions.name: get-mentions

max_results.description: Number of mentions to return (5-100, default 10)

actions.description: Add an emoji reaction to a specific Slack message
actions.name: add-reaction

channel_id.description: The channel ID where the message is located. Example: "C1234567890"

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the message to react to. Example: "1234567890.123456"

emoji_name.description: The name of the emoji to use (without colons). Example: "thumbsup"

actions.description: Create a new public or private Slack channel by name; does not create DMs or other conversation types.
actions.name: create-conversation

name.description: Name of the channel to create. Must be lowercase, contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores, and be 80 characters or less.

is_private.description: Whether the channel should be private. Defaults to false (public channel).

id.description: The unique identifier of the channel.

name.description: The normalized name of the channel.

created.description: Unix timestamp when the channel was created.

creator.description: User ID of the channel creator.

actions.description: Delete a message from a channel
actions.name: delete-message

channel_id.description: Channel ID containing the message. Example: "C1234567890"

message_ts.description: Timestamp of the message to delete. Example: "1405894322.002768"

actions.description: Look up a user by email address
actions.name: find-user-by-email

email.description: Email address of the user to look up. Example: "user@example.com"

actions.description: Retrieve conversation details including topic, purpose, and membership state
actions.name: get-channel-info

channel_id.description: Slack channel ID to retrieve information about. Example: "C012AB3CD"

is_channel.description: Whether this is a public channel

is_group.description: Whether this is a private channel

is_im.description: Whether this is a direct message

is_mpim.description: Whether this is a multi-person direct message

is_general.description: Whether this is the general channel

created.description: Unix timestamp when the channel was created

creator.description: User ID of the channel creator

num_members.description: Number of members in the channel (if available)

actions.description: Fetch paginated message history for a conversation within optional time bounds
actions.name: get-conversation-history

channel_id.description: The conversation ID to fetch history for. Example: "C1234567890"

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

oldest.description: Only messages after this Unix timestamp will be included. Example: "1512085950.000216"

latest.description: Only messages before this Unix timestamp will be included. Example: "1512104434.000490"

inclusive.description: Include messages with oldest or latest timestamps in results. Defaults to false.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return (max 999). Defaults to 100.

actions.description: Fetch paginated thread replies and parent message for a conversation thread
actions.name: get-thread-replies

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel/conversation containing the thread. Example: "C1234567890"

thread_ts.description: The timestamp of the parent message in the thread. Example: "1234567890.123456"

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return per page. Default: 100.

messages.description: Array of messages in the thread, including parent and replies

next_cursor.description: Pagination cursor for next page. Omitted if no more pages.

has_more.description: Whether there are more messages to fetch

actions.description: Retrieve a user's account details, including profile and avatar fields
actions.name: get-user-info

actions.description: Join a public or private channel and return its conversation details
actions.name: join-channel

channel_id.description: ID of the channel to join. Example: C061EG9SL

actions.description: List Slack channels the bot has access to
actions.name: list-channels

types.description: Comma-separated channel types: public_channel, private_channel (default: public_channel)

actions.description: List Slack conversations with optional type filters and cursor pagination.
actions.name: list-conversations

types.description: Comma-separated list of conversation types to filter by. Options: public_channel, private_channel, mpim, im. Default: public_channel.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

limit.description: Maximum number of conversations to return (1-200). Default: 100.

actions.description: List all items pinned in a specific channel
actions.name: list-pins

channel_id.description: The channel ID to list pinned items for. Example: "C1234567890"

created.description: Unix timestamp when the item was pinned

created_by.description: User ID who pinned the item

channel.description: Channel ID where the item is pinned

items.description: List of pinned items in the channel

actions.description: List all users in the workspace
actions.name: list-users

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

next_cursor.description: Pagination cursor for the next page. Omitted if no more pages.

actions.description: Move a conversation's read cursor to a specific message timestamp
actions.name: mark-as-read

channel_id.description: The channel ID to mark as read. Example: "C02MB5ZABA7"

message_ts.description: Timestamp of the message to mark as read. Example: "1234567890.123456"

actions.description: Post a message to a channel, DM, or thread
actions.name: post-message

channel.description: Channel, private group, or IM channel ID to send message to. Example: "C1234567890"

text.description: Text of the message to send

thread_ts.description: Timestamp of parent message to reply in thread. Example: "1234567890.123456"

channel.description: ID of the channel the message was sent to

bot_id.description: ID of the bot if sent by bot

message.description: The message object that was sent

actions.description: Schedule a Slack message to a channel or thread, subject to Slack's 120-day scheduling limit.
actions.name: schedule-message

channel_id.description: The channel to post to. Example: "C02MB5ZABA7"

post_at.description: Unix timestamp for when to post. Example: 1735689600

thread_ts.description: Optional thread timestamp to post in a thread. Example: "1234567890.123456"

actions.description: Search workspace files with pagination
actions.name: search-files

count.description: Number of items to return per page. Max 100. Default: 20

page.description: Page number of results to return. Default: 1

sort.description: Sort by score or timestamp. Default: score

sort_dir.description: Sort direction: ascending or descending. Default: desc

highlight.description: Enable query highlight markers in results. Default: false

actions.description: Search messages across Slack channels
actions.name: search-messages

query.description: Search query (supports Slack search operators like in:#channel, from:@user)

sort.description: Sort by relevance or recency (default: score)

actions.description: Send a message visible only to one user in a channel
actions.name: send-ephemeral-message

channel_id.description: Channel ID to send the ephemeral message to. Example: "C1234567890"

user_id.description: User ID to send the ephemeral message to. The user must be in the specified channel. Example: "U1234567890"

text.description: Text of the message to send. Supports Slack formatting.

thread_ts.description: Thread timestamp to reply to a specific thread. Example: "1234567890.123456"

actions.description: Send a message to a channel
actions.name: send-message

channel_id.description: Channel ID to send the message to. Example: "C1234567890"

text.description: Text content of the message to send. Example: "Hello world"

ts.description: Timestamp ID of the sent message

actions.description: Update a channel's purpose text for a conversation
actions.name: set-channel-purpose

channel_id.description: Channel ID to set the purpose for. Example: "C1234567890"

purpose.description: The new purpose text for the channel.

success.description: Whether the purpose was successfully updated

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel that was updated

purpose.description: The new purpose that was set

actions.description: Set the topic of a channel
actions.name: set-channel-topic

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel to set the topic for. Example: "C12345678"

topic.description: The new topic string. Does not support formatting or linkification. Example: "Apply topically for best effects"

actions.description: Edit an existing message in a Slack channel
actions.name: update-message

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel containing the message to update. Example: "C1234567890"

message_ts.description: The timestamp of the message to update. Example: "1401383885.000061"

text.description: The updated text of the message. Example: "Updated message text"

as_user.description: Pass true to update the message as the authenticated user. Bot users in this context are considered authed users. Default: true

link_names.description: Find and link channel names and usernames. Defaults to false. To use this, you need parse set to "full".

parse.description: Change how messages are treated. Defaults to "client" which attempts to discover links. Use "none" to treat text literally, "full" for full parsing with link_names.

unfurl_links.description: Pass false to disable unfurling of links.

unfurl_media.description: Pass false to disable unfurling of media content.

reply_broadcast.description: Used to reply to a thread only and not to the channel. Pass true to reply to the channel as well.

blocks.description: A JSON array of blocks to use as the message content. When blocks is provided, text becomes the fallback text for notifications.

attachments.description: A JSON array of legacy attachments. Not recommended for new apps, use blocks instead.

ok.description: Whether the API call was successful

channel.description: The ID of the channel where the message was updated

ts.description: The timestamp of the updated message

text.description: The updated text of the message

message.description: Full message object containing updated message details

actions.description: Adds new child blocks to a page or block (max 100 blocks).
actions.name: append-block-children

block_id.description: The ID of the block or page to append to. Example: "2b6ce298-3121-80ae-bfe1-f8984b993639"

children.description: Array of block objects to append (max 100).

actions.description: Moves a page to trash by setting archived to true.
actions.name: archive-page

page_id.description: The ID of the page to archive. Example: "2b6ce298-3121-80ae-bfe1-f8984b993639"

actions.description: Adds a comment to a page or existing discussion thread.
actions.name: create-comment

page_id.description: Page ID to add comment to. Example: "2b6ce298-3121-80ae-bfe1-f8984b993639"

rich_text.description: Comment content as rich text array.

discussion_id.description: Discussion thread ID to reply to.

actions.description: Creates a new page as child of a page or database with optional content blocks.
actions.name: create-page

parent.description: Parent page or database. Must include either page_id or database_id.

properties.description: Page properties. For pages with page parent, use title property. For database parents, use database property schema.

children.description: Array of block objects to add as page content.

icon.description: Page icon as emoji or external URL.

cover.description: Page cover image as external URL.

actions.description: Retrieves unresolved comments from a page or block.
actions.name: list-comments

block_id.description: The ID of the page or block. Example: "2b6ce298-3121-80ae-bfe1-f8984b993639"

actions.description: Gets paginated list of all workspace users excluding guests.
actions.name: list-users

actions.description: Retrieves filtered and sorted pages from a database with pagination.
actions.name: query-database

database_id.description: The ID of the database to query. Example: "2b6ce298-3121-8079-a497-d3eca16d875c"

actions.description: Gets paginated list of child blocks within a block or page.
actions.name: retrieve-block-children

block_id.description: The ID of the block or page. Example: "2b6ce298-3121-80ae-bfe1-f8984b993639"

actions.description: Gets database schema and column structure.
actions.name: retrieve-database

database_id.description: The ID of the database to retrieve. Example: "2b6ce298-3121-8079-a497-d3eca16d875c"

actions.description: Fetches page properties and metadata by page ID.
actions.name: retrieve-page

page_id.description: The ID of the page to retrieve. Example: "2b6ce298-3121-80ae-bfe1-f8984b993639"

actions.description: Searches only pages shared with the integration.
actions.name: search-pages

query.description: Text to search for in page titles.

actions.description: Modifies page properties, icon, cover, or archived status.
actions.name: update-page

page_id.description: The ID of the page to update. Example: "2b6ce298-3121-80ae-bfe1-f8984b993639"

icon.description: Page icon as emoji or external URL.

cover.description: Page cover image as external URL.

archived.description: Set to true to archive the page.

actions.description: Append values to the end of a spreadsheet table
actions.name: append-values-to-spreadsheet

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. Example: "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms"

range.description: The A1 notation of a range to search for a logical table of data. Example: "Sheet1!A1" or "Sheet1"

values.description: The values to append to the spreadsheet. Each inner array represents a row of data.

valueInputOption.description: How the input data should be interpreted. "RAW": The values will be parsed as if the user typed them into the UI. "USER_ENTERED": The values will be parsed as if the user typed them into the UI, but formulas will be calculated.

insertDataOption.description: How the input data should be inserted. "OVERWRITE": Overwrite existing data. "INSERT_ROWS": Insert new rows.

majorDimension.description: The major dimension of the values. "ROWS": Values are organized by row. "COLUMNS": Values are organized by column.

actions.description: Get values from multiple ranges
actions.name: batch-get-values

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve data from. Example: "1abc123xyz"

ranges.description: The A1 notation or R1C1 notation of the ranges to retrieve values from. Example: ["Sheet1!A1:D5", "Sheet2!B2:C4"]

majorDimension.description: The major dimension that results should use. Defaults to ROWS.

valueRenderOption.description: How values should be rendered in the output.

dateTimeRenderOption.description: How dates, times, and durations should be represented in the output.

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet the data was retrieved from.

range.description: The range the values cover, in A1 notation.

majorDimension.description: The major dimension of the values.

values.description: The data that was read. Array of arrays representing rows/columns, with each cell value being a string, number, boolean, or null.

valueRanges.description: The values of the ranges requested.

actions.description: Clear values from a range, preserving formatting
actions.name: clear-values

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. Example: "1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j"

range.description: The A1 notation or R1C1 notation of the values to clear. Example: "Sheet1!A1:D10"

actions.description: Insert a new row at a given index in a Google Sheet
actions.name: create-spreadsheet-row

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet. Example: "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms"

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet (can be found via the API). Example: 687284948

sheetName.description: The name of the sheet for A1 notation. Example: "Sheet1"

rowIndex.description: The index where to insert the row (0-based, where 0 is the first row). Example: 5

values.description: Array of values to insert in the new row. Example: ["John", "Doe", "john@example.com"]

actions.description: Create a new spreadsheet
actions.name: create-spreadsheet

sheets.description: Array of sheets to create in the spreadsheet

spreadsheetId.description: The unique ID of the created spreadsheet

spreadsheetUrl.description: The URL to view the spreadsheet in Google Sheets

actions.description: Get values from a spreadsheet range
actions.name: get-values

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve data from. Example: "1aBcD..."

range.description: The A1 notation or R1C1 notation of the range to retrieve values from. Example: "Sheet1!A1:C10" or "Sheet1"

values.description: The data values in the range, as a 2D array where each inner array represents a row

actions.description: List Google Sheets spreadsheets accessible to the user
actions.name: list-spreadsheets

query.description: Search query to filter spreadsheets by name

actions.description: Update values in a spreadsheet range
actions.name: update-values

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. Example: "1abc123def456ghi"

range.description: The A1 notation of the values to update. Example: "Sheet1!A1:B2"

values.description: The data to write, as an array of arrays. Each inner array represents a row. Example: [["A1", "B1"], ["A2", "B2"]]

valueInputOption.description: How the input data should be interpreted. RAW = values as-is, USER_ENTERED = parsed as if typed into the UI. Default: USER_ENTERED

majorDimension.description: The major dimension of the values. ROWS = each inner array is a row, COLUMNS = each inner array is a column. Default: ROWS

includeValuesInResponse.description: If true, the response includes the updated cell values. Default: false

actions.description: Append or update a row of values in a Google Sheet
actions.name: upsert-row

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. Example: "1aBcD..."

range.description: The A1 notation of the range to search for existing data and append to. Example: "Sheet1!A1:E" or "Sheet1"

values.description: The row values to upsert. Example: ["Name", "Email", "Phone"]

keyColumn.description: The column index (0-based) to use as the key for matching existing rows. If not provided, always appends.

keyValue.description: The value to match in the key column for updating an existing row. Required if key_column is provided.

actions.description: Add an attendee to an existing calendar event
actions.name: add-attendee

calendarId.description: Calendar ID. Use "primary" for the default calendar. Example: "primary"

eventId.description: Event ID to add attendee to. Example: "abc123xyz"

attendeeEmail.description: Email address of the attendee to add. Example: "attendee@example.com"

attendeeName.description: Display name of the attendee (optional). Example: "John Doe"

optional.description: Whether the attendee is optional (optional). Default: false

responseStatus.description: Response status of the attendee (optional). Default: "needsAction"

actions.description: Create an all-day calendar event using start and end dates
actions.name: create-all-day-event

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use "primary" for the primary calendar.

actions.description: Create a calendar event
actions.name: create-event

calendarId.description: Calendar ID to create the event in. Example: "primary" or a calendar ID string

dateTime.description: Start time in ISO 8601 format. Example: "2024-03-15T09:00:00-07:00"

timeZone.description: Time zone for the start time. Example: "America/Los_Angeles"

dateTime.description: End time in ISO 8601 format. Example: "2024-03-15T10:00:00-07:00"

timeZone.description: Time zone for the end time. Example: "America/Los_Angeles"

email.description: Email address of the attendee. Example: "attendee@example.com"

minutes.description: Minutes before the event to trigger the reminder

recurrence.description: Recurrence rules (RRULE, EXRULE, RDATE, EXDATE). Example: ["RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO"]

htmlLink.description: Link to the event in Google Calendar

actions.description: Create a recurring event with supplied start, end, and RRULE values
actions.name: create-recurring-event

start.description: Event start time in RFC3339 format. Example: "2024-03-15T09:00:00-07:00"

end.description: Event end time in RFC3339 format. Example: "2024-03-15T10:00:00-07:00"

timezone.description: Timezone for the event. Defaults to "UTC". Example: "America/Los_Angeles"

actions.description: Delete a calendar event
actions.name: delete-event

actions.description: Query free/busy data and return gaps meeting a minimum duration
actions.name: find-free-slots

calendarIds.description: List of calendar IDs to check for free/busy information. Example: ["primary", "work@example.com"]

timeMin.description: Start of the time range in RFC3339 format. Example: "2024-03-15T09:00:00Z"

timeMax.description: End of the time range in RFC3339 format. Example: "2024-03-15T17:00:00Z"

durationMinutes.description: Minimum duration in minutes for a free slot to be returned. Example: 30

start.description: Start time of the free slot in RFC3339 format

end.description: End time of the free slot in RFC3339 format

durationMinutes.description: Duration of the free slot in minutes

freeSlots.description: List of free time slots meeting the minimum duration

actions.description: Get an event by ID from Google Calendar
actions.name: get-event

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use "primary" for the primary calendar of the currently logged in user.

actions.description: List calendars in the user's calendar list
actions.name: list-calendar-list

cursor.description: Page token for pagination. Omit for first page.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of calendars to return. Default: 100.

actions.description: List events on a calendar
actions.name: list-events

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use "primary" for the primary calendar or a calendar ID from calendarList.list.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor (nextPageToken from previous response). Omit for first page.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of events to return per page (1-2500, default 250).

timeMin.description: Lower bound (exclusive) for an event's end time filter (RFC3339 timestamp).

timeMax.description: Upper bound (exclusive) for an event's start time filter (RFC3339 timestamp).

q.description: Free text search terms to find matching events.

singleEvents.description: Whether to expand recurring events into single instances.

nextPageToken.description: Pagination cursor for next page. Omitted if no more pages.

actions.description: List upcoming events from now, ordered by start time
actions.name: list-upcoming-events

calendarId.description: Calendar ID. Use "primary" for the main calendar or a specific calendar ID. Example: "primary"

cursor.description: Pagination token from previous response. Omit for first page.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return per page (1-2500). Default: 250

timeMin.description: RFC3339 timestamp to fetch events from (e.g., "2026-03-12T00:00:00Z"). Defaults to current time if not provided.

htmlLink.description: Link to event in Google Calendar

updated.description: When the event was last updated

nextPageToken.description: Token for fetching the next page. Omitted if no more pages.

actions.description: Return free/busy blocks for one or more calendars in a time range
actions.name: query-free-busy

timeMin.description: The start of the interval for the query formatted as per RFC3339. Example: "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"

timeMax.description: The end of the interval for the query formatted as per RFC3339. Example: "2024-01-02T00:00:00Z"

timeZone.description: Time zone used in the response. The default is UTC. Example: "UTC"

groupExpansionMax.description: Maximal number of calendar identifiers to be provided for a single group. Maximum value is 100.

calendarExpansionMax.description: Maximal number of calendars for which FreeBusy information is to be provided. Maximum value is 50.

id.description: The identifier of a calendar or a group

items.description: List of calendars and/or groups to query

actions.description: Create an event from a text string
actions.name: quick-add-event

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use "primary" for the primary calendar. Example: "primary"

text.description: The text describing the event to be created. Example: "Meeting with John tomorrow at 2pm"

sendUpdates.description: Guests who should receive notifications about the creation of the new event. Acceptable values: "all", "externalOnly", "none".

id.description: The unique ID of the event.

start.description: The start time of the event.

end.description: The end time of the event.

htmlLink.description: A link to the event in Google Calendar.

created.description: The creation time of the event.

updated.description: The last modification time of the event.

actions.description: Fetch an event, remove an attendee by email, and patch attendees
actions.name: remove-attendee

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use "primary" for the primary calendar of the currently logged in user.

attendeeEmail.description: Email address of the attendee to remove from the event.

actions.description: Update a calendar event
actions.name: update-event

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use "primary" for the main calendar

date.description: Date in yyyy-mm-dd format for all-day events

dateTime.description: DateTime in RFC3339 format for timed events

date.description: Date in yyyy-mm-dd format for all-day events

dateTime.description: DateTime in RFC3339 format for timed events

sendUpdates.description: Who should receive notifications about the event update

date.description: Date in yyyy-mm-dd format for all-day events

dateTime.description: DateTime in RFC3339 format for timed events

date.description: Date in yyyy-mm-dd format for all-day events

dateTime.description: DateTime in RFC3339 format for timed events

actions.description: Search for files by name or query in Google Drive
actions.name: find-file

query.description: Search query string. Uses Google Drive search query syntax. Example: "name contains 'report'" or "mimeType = 'application/pdf'". If not provided, returns all files.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor (nextPageToken) from previous response. Omit for first page.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of files to return per page. Default is 100.

nextPageToken.description: Pagination cursor for the next page. Omitted if no more results.

totalResults.description: Total number of files returned in this page

actions.description: Search for a folder by name or query
actions.name: find-folder

name.description: Folder name or search query to find folders by name. Example: "Test Folder Alpha"

actions.description: Create a new folder in Google Drive
actions.name: create-folder

name.description: The name of the new folder. Example: "My New Folder"

parentId.description: The ID of the parent folder where the new folder will be created. If omitted, the folder is created in the root of the drive. Example: "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms"

id.description: The unique identifier of the created folder.

name.description: The name of the created folder.

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the folder (always application/vnd.google-apps.folder).

createdTime.description: The timestamp when the folder was created.

actions.description: Upload plain text or base64 file content up to 5 MB, optionally into a folder with a description; defaults to root
actions.name: upload-document

name.description: The name of the file to create. Example: "document.txt"

content.description: The file content as plain text or base64 encoded string

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file. Example: "text/plain", "application/pdf"

isBase64.description: Whether the content is base64 encoded. Defaults to false

folderId.description: The ID of the folder to upload the file into. If not provided, defaults to root. Example: "1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h"

id.description: The ID of the created file

name.description: The name of the created file

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file

webViewLink.description: A link for opening the file in a relevant Google editor or viewer

webContentLink.description: A link for downloading the content of the file in a browser

actions.description: Copy a file to a destination
actions.name: copy-file

fileId.description: The ID of the file to copy. Example: "123abc"

name.description: The new name for the copied file. If not provided, the original name is used.

destinationFolderId.description: The ID of the folder where the copy should be placed. If not provided, the copy is placed in the same folder as the original.

id.description: The ID of the copied file

name.description: The name of the copied file

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the copied file

createdTime.description: The creation time of the copied file (RFC 3339)

actions.description: Move a file to a different folder
actions.name: move-file

fileId.description: The ID of the file to move. Example: "1mD3ukEAmRqo8u0RF_Cr6IJl9f_uWTYH03vesDhB5Svw"

fromFolderId.description: The ID of the current parent folder. Example: "1SpnQKJHqNDh-qhbj_zGD2aIm-G-RKC_k"

toFolderId.description: The ID of the destination folder. Example: "1Bl1rB7hkBbdzmKUka3zSj0bhAK3pGypD"

actions.description: Delete a file or folder from Google Drive
actions.name: delete-file

fileId.description: The ID of the file or folder to delete. Example: "1aBcDeFgHiJkLmNoPqRsTuVwXyZ123456"

success.description: Whether the file was successfully deleted

fileId.description: The ID of the deleted file

actions.description: List immediate files and folders for a folder ID, or root when omitted, with cursor pagination and shared-drive support.
actions.name: list-files-non-unified

folderId.description: Folder ID to list contents. Omit for root folder.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor (pageToken) from previous response. Omit for first page.

limit.description: Maximum number of files to return. Default: 100.

includeSharedDrives.description: Include items from shared drives. Default: false.

nextPageToken.description: Cursor for next page. Omitted if no more pages.

totalCount.description: Total number of files in this page.

actions.description: Send an email using Gmail
actions.name: send-email

headers.description: Optional additional email headers (e.g. Cc, Bcc, Reply-To)

actions.description: Fetch the content of a Gmail attachment
actions.name: fetch-attachment

messageId.description: The ID of the message containing the attachment

attachmentId.description: The ID of the attachment to fetch

actions.description: List Gmail messages with optional filtering by label or search query
actions.name: list-emails

actions.description: Read a specific Gmail message including body, headers, and attachment metadata
actions.name: read-email

actions.description: Search Gmail messages using Gmail search syntax
actions.name: search-emails

actions.description: Reply to an existing Gmail message in the same thread
actions.name: reply-to-email

actions.description: Share a Google Drive file or folder with a specific person
actions.name: share-file

actions.description: Get detailed metadata and permissions for a Google Drive file
actions.name: get-file-metadata
